name: Cutover Readiness Audit

phase19_0_runtime_packaging: external data dir and explicit DB path
phase19_1_real_data_canary: Pi v16 DB copied before Go canary
Go sidecar read copied v16 DB only
Pi sidecar smoke covered healthz, test, categories, tags, notes list, note detail, and note 404
Windows embedded frontend smoke captured GET-only API calls
runtime metrics captured for Python and Go
Go logs showed GET startup/request activity only
phase19_2_promotion_gate: Go route surface locked to GET read-only endpoints
Python remains runtime owner and rollback path
Go migrations and default frontend cutover remain blocked
phase19_3_read_routing_proof: Go base URL restricted to localhost, 127.0.0.1, or ::1 with explicit port
only validated GET read surface may proxy
invalid base URL and unavailable sidecar fail open to Python
GET /api/system/go-read-routing reports switch status and fallback owner

blocking_gaps_before_any_cutover: No production service-level cutover plan exists
No live Pi service replacement has been approved or performed
No long-running read-routing soak has been recorded
No Caddy/systemd read-routing deployment contract exists
No rollback drill from Go-routed reads back to Python service has been recorded
Go still does not own POST / PUT / DELETE
Go still does not own attachments, export, cleanup, server maintenance, or migrations
Go still does not write or migrate the production DB
Python remains required for all write and file workflows

allowed_next_step.title: Read-only Service-level Cutover Plan
allowed_next_step.scope: plan a separately approved read-only service-level cutover or soak test with explicit rollback, deployment, monitoring, and no write/file/migration ownership change
must_not_include: attachments, export, cleanup, or server maintenance

not_authorized_by_19_4: Changing frontend default API target in production
Writing to production knowledge.db from Go
